commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer approval-boundary judgment and contributor real behavior proof; there is no narrow automation repair until that direction is accepted.; Needs attention: The diff touches a desktop-control approval boundary by accepting non-object Computer Use schemas, so it needs maintainer signoff and real-flow proof before merge. (sha=ed91eb116612909896afe14e6fb912379d38b54f)
commands.review_summary:
The branch normalizes non-object Computer Use elicitation schemas to an empty object schema, updates focused bridge coverage, and adds `/approve` to the Slack native command manifest docs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level seam: current main rejects exact-server Computer Use form elicitations when `requestedSchema` is not a JSON object. No high-confidence live Slack/Computer Use reproduction is present in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer approval-boundary judgment and contributor real behavior proof; there is no narrow automation repair until that direction is accepted.

target.title: fix(codex): bridge computer use elicitations
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Computer Use MCP elicitations could depend on an exact configured server name, so app-server bridge approval handling missed real `computer-use` server-name variants.
- Solution: Detect Computer Use elicitations by the `computer-use` server-name fragment and bridge them through plugin approval handling without passing a per-run exact server name.
- What changed: Computer Use schema-less elicitations normalize to an empty object schema, MCP elicitation delegation only turns on for Computer Use server names, the app-server bridge tests cover the new behavior, and Slack docs list `/approve`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No native Slack UI behavior, gateway approval transport, or non-Computer Use plugin approval policy is changed.

## Motivation

- This preserves the cherry-picked bridge elicitation fix on top of current `main` without carrying old-base code churn.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Computer Use app-server MCP elicitations are routed into plugin approvals when the server name contains `computer-use`, including elicitations without an object schema.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree unit/seam tests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): `Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 227 passed (227)` and `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` exited cleanly.
- Observed result after fix: The focused app-server bridge suites pass with Computer Use elicitations bridged through plugin approval handling.
- What was not tested: Live Slack plus Computer Use roundtrip was not rerun in this cherry-pick PR.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): N/A.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The bridge logic coupled Computer Use approval elicitations to an exact configured MCP server name and rejected non-object schemas before plugin approval could handle them.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Coverage did not lock the real `computer-use` server-name path with schema-less elicitation requests.
- Contributing context (if known): This change is a current-main cherry-pick of the bridge elicitation fix.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts`; `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Computer Use elicitations bridge by `computer-use` server-name fragment and normalize missing schemas to an empty object schema.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is in the app-server bridge decision path, so focused app-server tests exercise the contract without requiring a live channel.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing bridge tests covered approval roundtrip mapping, but not this server-name/schema shape.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Slack channel docs now list `/approve` among app commands. Computer Use app-server approval elicitations are more reliably bridged into plugin approval handling.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Computer Use elicitation] -> [exact configured server-name check] -> [may skip bridge]

After:
[Computer Use elicitation] -> [computer-use server-name fragment check] -> [plugin approval bridge]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node via `scripts/run-vitest.mjs`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Codex app-server / Computer Use MCP elicitation bridge
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`.
2. Run `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.

### Expected

- Focused app-server bridge tests pass and the diff has no whitespace errors.

### Actual

- `Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 227 passed (227)`.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` exited cleanly.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Focused app-server bridge tests for Computer Use elicitation routing and run-attempt delegation.
- Edge cases checked: Missing Computer Use schema normalizes to an empty object schema; non-Computer Use server names remain unhandled by the Computer Use bridge.
- What you did **not** verify: Live Slack plus Computer Use roundtrip for this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Computer Use matching by server-name fragment could include namespaced Computer Use MCP server names.
  - Mitigation: The bridge only applies to current thread/turn elicitation requests and still routes through plugin approval before accepting.
target.author: kevinslin
